award_info.name: Giải Nobel Văn chương
award_info.description: Giải thưởng văn học danh giá nhất thế giới, được trao hàng năm cho tác giả có đóng góp xuất sắc cho văn học thế giới

laureates.name: László Krasznahorkai
laureates.motivation: for his compelling and visionary oeuvre that, in the midst of apocalyptic terror, reaffirms the power of art

laureates.name: Han Kang
laureates.motivation: for her intense poetic prose that confronts historical traumas and exposes the fragility of human life

laureates.name: Jon Fosse
laureates.motivation: for his innovative plays and prose which give voice to the unsayable

laureates.name: Annie Ernaux
laureates.motivation: for the courage and clinical acuity with which she uncovers the roots, estrangements and collective restraints of personal memory

laureates.name: Abdulrazak Gurnah
laureates.motivation: for his uncompromising and compassionate penetration of the effects of colonialism and the fate of the refugee in the gulf between cultures and continents

laureates.name: Louise Glück
laureates.motivation: for her unmistakable poetic voice that with austere beauty makes individual existence universal

laureates.name: Peter Handke
laureates.motivation: for an influential work that with linguistic ingenuity has explored the periphery and the specificity of human experience

laureates.name: Olga Tokarczuk
laureates.motivation: for a narrative imagination that with encyclopedic passion represents the crossing of boundaries as a form of life

laureates.name: Kazuo Ishiguro
laureates.motivation: who, in novels of great emotional force, has uncovered the abyss beneath our illusory sense of connection with the world

laureates.name: Bob Dylan
laureates.motivation: for having created new poetic expressions within the great American song tradition

laureates.name: Svetlana Alexievich
laureates.motivation: for her polyphonic writings, a monument to suffering and courage in our time

laureates.name: Patrick Modiano
laureates.motivation: for the art of memory with which he has evoked the most ungraspable human destinies and uncovered the life-world of the Occupation

laureates.name: Alice Munro
laureates.motivation: master of the contemporary short story

laureates.name: Mo Yan
laureates.motivation: who with hallucinatory realism merges folk tales, history and the contemporary

2011.name: Tomas Tranströmer
2011.motivation: because, through his condensed, translucent images, he gives us fresh access to reality

laureates.name: Mario Vargas Llosa
laureates.motivation: for his cartography of structures of power and his trenchant images of the individual's resistance, revolt, and defeat

2009.name: Herta Müller
2009.motivation: who, with the concentration of poetry and the frankness of prose, depicts the landscape of the dispossessed

2008.name: Jean-Marie Gustave Le Clézio
2008.motivation: author of new departures, poetic adventure and sensual ecstasy, explorer of a humanity beyond and below the reigning civilization

2007.name: Doris Lessing
2007.motivation: that epicist of the female experience, who with scepticism, fire and visionary power has subjected a divided civilisation to scrutiny

laureates.name: Orhan Pamuk
laureates.motivation: who in the quest for the melancholic soul of his native city has discovered new symbols for the clash and interlacing of cultures

2005.name: Harold Pinter
2005.motivation: who in his plays uncovers the precipice under everyday prattle and forces entry into oppression's closed rooms

2004.name: Elfriede Jelinek
2004.motivation: for her musical flow of voices and counter-voices in novels and plays that with extraordinary linguistic zeal reveal the absurdity of society's clichés and their subjugating power

2003.name: J. M. Coetzee
2003.motivation: who in innumerable guises portrays the surprising involvement of the outsider

laureates.name: Imre Kertész
laureates.motivation: for writing that upholds the fragile experience of the individual against the barbaric arbitrariness of history

laureates.name: V. S. Naipaul
laureates.motivation: for having united perceptive narrative and incorruptible scrutiny in works that compel us to see the presence of suppressed histories

laureates.name: Gao Xingjian
laureates.motivation: for an oeuvre of universal validity, bitter insights and linguistic ingenuity, which has opened new paths for the Chinese novel and drama

laureates.name: Günter Grass
laureates.motivation: whose frolicsome black fables portray the forgotten face of history

laureates.name: José Saramago
laureates.motivation: who with parables sustained by imagination, compassion and irony continually enables us once again to apprehend an elusory reality

laureates.name: Dario Fo
laureates.motivation: who emulates the jesters of the Middle Ages in scourging authority and upholding the dignity of the downtrodden

laureates.name: Wisława Szymborska
laureates.motivation: for poetry that with ironic precision allows the historical and biological context to come to light in fragments of human reality

laureates.name: Seamus Heaney
laureates.motivation: for works of lyrical beauty and ethical depth, which exalt everyday miracles and the living past

laureates.name: Kenzaburō Ōe
laureates.motivation: who with poetic force creates an imagined world, where life and myth condense to form a disconcerting picture of the human predicament today

laureates.name: Toni Morrison
laureates.motivation: who in novels characterized by visionary force and poetic import, gives life to an essential aspect of American reality

laureates.name: Derek Walcott
laureates.motivation: for a poetic oeuvre of great luminosity, sustained by a historical vision, the outcome of a multicultural commitment

laureates.name: Nadine Gordimer
laureates.motivation: who through her magnificent epic writing has – in the words of Alfred Nobel – been of very great benefit to humanity

laureates.name: Octavio Paz
laureates.motivation: for impassioned writing with wide horizons, characterized by sensuous intelligence and humanistic integrity

laureates.name: Camilo José Cela
laureates.motivation: for a rich and intensive prose, which with restrained compassion forms a challenging vision of man's vulnerability

laureates.name: Naguib Mahfouz
laureates.motivation: who, through works rich in nuance – now clear-sightedly realistic, now evocatively ambiguous – has formed an Arabian narrative art that applies to all mankind

laureates.name: Joseph Brodsky
laureates.motivation: for an all-embracing authorship, imbued with clarity of thought and poetic intensity

laureates.name: Wole Soyinka
laureates.motivation: who in a wide cultural perspective and with poetic overtones fashions the drama of existence

laureates.name: Claude Simon
laureates.motivation: who in his novel combines the poet's and the painter's creativeness with a deepened awareness of time in the depiction of the human condition

laureates.name: Jaroslav Seifert
laureates.motivation: for his poetry, which endowed with freshness, and rich inventiveness provides a liberating image of the indomitable spirit and versatility of man

laureates.name: William Golding
laureates.motivation: for his novels, which with the perspicuity of realistic narrative art and the diversity and universality of myth, illuminate the human condition in the world of today

laureates.name: Gabriel García Márquez
laureates.motivation: for his novels and short stories, in which the fantastic and the realistic are combined in a richly composed world of imagination, reflecting a continent's life and conflicts

laureates.name: Elias Canetti
laureates.motivation: for writings marked by a broad outlook, a wealth of ideas and artistic power

laureates.name: Czesław Miłosz
laureates.motivation: who with uncompromising clear-sightedness voices man's exposed condition in a world of severe conflicts

laureates.name: Odysseas Elytis
laureates.motivation: for his poetry, which, against the background of Greek tradition, depicts with sensuous strength and intellectual clear-sightedness modern man's struggle for freedom and creativeness

laureates.name: Isaac Bashevis Singer
laureates.motivation: for his impassioned narrative art which, with roots in a Polish-Jewish cultural tradition, brings universal human conditions to life

laureates.name: Vicente Aleixandre
laureates.motivation: for a creative poetic writing, which illuminates man's condition in the cosmos and in present-day society, at the same time representing the great renewal of the traditions of Spanish poetry between the wars

laureates.name: Saul Bellow
laureates.motivation: for the human understanding and subtle analysis of contemporary culture that are combined in his work

laureates.name: Eugenio Montale
laureates.motivation: for his distinctive poetry, which, with great artistic sensitivity, has interpreted human values under the sign of an outlook on life with no illusions

laureates.name: Eyvind Johnson
laureates.motivation: for a narrative art, farseeing in lands and ages, in the service of freedom

laureates.name: Harry Martinson
laureates.motivation: for writings that catch the dewdrop and reflect the cosmos

laureates.name: Patrick White
laureates.motivation: for an epic and psychological narrative art, which has introduced a new continent into literature

laureates.name: Heinrich Böll
laureates.motivation: for his writing, which through its combination of a broad perspective on his time and a sensitive skill in characterization has contributed to a renewal of German literature

laureates.name: Pablo Neruda
laureates.motivation: for a poetry that with the action of an elemental force brings alive a continent's destiny and dreams

laureates.name: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
laureates.motivation: for the ethical force with which he has pursued the indispensable traditions of Russian literature

laureates.name: Samuel Beckett
laureates.motivation: for his writing, which – in new forms for the novel and drama – in the destitution of modern man acquires its elevation

laureates.name: Yasunari Kawabata
laureates.motivation: for his narrative mastery, which with great sensibility expresses the essence of the Japanese mind

laureates.name: Miguel Ángel Asturias
laureates.motivation: for his vivid literary achievement, deep-rooted in the national traits and traditions of Indian peoples of Latin America

laureates.name: Shmuel Yosef Agnon
laureates.motivation: for his profoundly characteristic narrative art with motifs from the life of the Jewish people

laureates.name: Nelly Sachs
laureates.motivation: for her outstanding lyrical and dramatic writing, which interprets Israel's destiny with touching strength

laureates.name: Mikhail Sholokhov
laureates.motivation: for the artistic power and integrity with which, in his epic of the Don, he has given expression to a historic phase in the life of the Russian people

laureates.name: Jean-Paul Sartre
laureates.motivation: for his work, which rich in ideas and filled with the spirit of freedom and the quest for truth, has exerted a far-reaching influence on our age

laureates.name: Giorgos Seferis
laureates.motivation: for his eminent lyrical writing, inspired by a deep feeling for the Hellenic world of culture

laureates.name: John Steinbeck
laureates.motivation: for his realistic and imaginative writings, combining as they do sympathetic humour and keen social perception

laureates.name: Ivo Andrić
laureates.motivation: for the epic force with which he has traced themes and depicted human destinies drawn from the history of his country

laureates.name: Saint-John Perse
laureates.motivation: for the soaring flight and the evocative imagery of his poetry, which in a visionary fashion reflects the conditions of our time

laureates.name: Salvatore Quasimodo
laureates.motivation: for his lyrical poetry, which with classical fire expresses the tragic experience of life in our own times

laureates.name: Boris Pasternak
laureates.motivation: for his important achievement both in contemporary lyrical poetry and in the field of the great Russian epic tradition
laureates.notes: Forced to decline by Soviet authorities

1958.notes: Forced to decline by Soviet authorities

laureates.name: Albert Camus
laureates.motivation: for his important literary production, which with clear-sighted earnestness illuminates the problems of the human conscience in our times

laureates.name: Juan Ramón Jiménez
laureates.motivation: for his lyrical poetry, which in Spanish language constitutes an example of high spirit and artistical purity

laureates.name: Halldór Laxness
laureates.motivation: for his vivid epic power, which has renewed the great narrative art of Iceland

laureates.name: Ernest Hemingway
laureates.motivation: for his mastery of the art of narrative, most recently demonstrated in The Old Man and the Sea, and for the influence that he has exerted on contemporary style

laureates.name: Winston Churchill
laureates.motivation: for his mastery of historical and biographical description as well as for brilliant oratory in defending exalted human values

laureates.name: François Mauriac
laureates.motivation: for the deep spiritual insight and the artistic intensity with which he has in his novels penetrated the drama of human life

laureates.name: Pär Lagerkvist
laureates.motivation: for the artistic vigour and true independence of mind with which he endeavours in his poetry to find answers to the eternal questions confronting mankind

laureates.name: Bertrand Russell
laureates.motivation: in recognition of his varied and significant writings in which he champions humanitarian ideals and freedom of thought

laureates.name: William Faulkner
laureates.motivation: for his powerful and artistically unique contribution to the modern American novel

laureates.name: T. S. Eliot
laureates.country: United Kingdom (born in the United States)
laureates.motivation: for his outstanding, pioneer contribution to present-day poetry

laureates.name: André Gide
laureates.motivation: for his comprehensive and artistically significant writings, in which human problems and conditions have been presented with a fearless love of truth and keen psychological insight

laureates.name: Hermann Hesse
laureates.motivation: for his inspired writings, which while growing in boldness and penetration, exemplify the classical humanitarian ideals and high qualities of style

laureates.name: Gabriela Mistral
laureates.motivation: for her lyric poetry, which inspired by powerful emotions, has made her name a symbol of the idealistic aspirations of the entire Latin American world

laureates.name: Johannes V. Jensen
laureates.motivation: for the rare strength and fertility of his poetic imagination with which is combined an intellectual curiosity of wide scope and a bold, freshly creative style

1943.notes: No Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded this year due to World War II

1942.notes: No Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded this year due to World War II

1941.notes: No Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded this year due to World War II

1940.notes: No Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded this year due to World War II and the German occupation of Norway

laureates.name: Frans Eemil Sillanpää
laureates.motivation: for his deep understanding of his country's peasantry and the exquisite art with which he has portrayed their way of life and their relationship with Nature

laureates.name: Pearl S. Buck
laureates.motivation: for her rich and truly epic descriptions of peasant life in China and for her biographical masterpieces

laureates.name: Roger Martin du Gard
laureates.motivation: for the artistic power and truth with which he has depicted human conflict as well as some fundamental aspects of contemporary life in his novel cycle Les Thibault

laureates.name: Eugene O'Neill
laureates.motivation: for the power, honesty and deep-felt emotions of his dramatic works, which embody an original concept of tragedy

1935.notes: No Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded this year as no candidate was deemed suitable by the Swedish Academy

laureates.name: Luigi Pirandello
laureates.motivation: for his bold and ingenious revival of dramatic and scenic art

laureates.name: Ivan Bunin
laureates.motivation: for the strict artistry with which he has carried on the classical Russian traditions in prose writing

laureates.name: John Galsworthy
laureates.motivation: for his distinguished art of narration, which takes its highest form in The Forsyte Saga

laureates.name: Erik Axel Karlfeldt
laureates.motivation: The poetry of Erik Axel Karlfeldt

laureates.name: Sinclair Lewis
laureates.motivation: for his vigorous and graphic art of description and his ability to create, with wit and humour, new types of characters

laureates.name: Thomas Mann
laureates.motivation: principally for his great novel, Buddenbrooks, which has won steadily increased recognition as one of the classic works of contemporary literature

laureates.name: Sigrid Undset
laureates.motivation: principally for her powerful descriptions of Northern life during the Middle Ages

laureates.name: Henri Bergson
laureates.motivation: in recognition of his rich and vitalizing ideas and the brilliant skill with which they have been presented

laureates.name: Grazia Deledda
laureates.motivation: for her idealistically inspired writings, which with plastic clarity picture the life on her native island and with depth and sympathy deal with human problems in general

laureates.name: George Bernard Shaw
laureates.motivation: for his work which is marked by both idealism and humanity, its stimulating satire often being infused with a singular poetic beauty

laureates.name: Władysław Reymont
laureates.motivation: for his great national epic, The Peasants

laureates.name: William Butler Yeats
laureates.motivation: for his always inspired poetry, which in a highly artistic form gives expression to the spirit of a whole nation

laureates.name: Jacinto Benavente
laureates.motivation: for the happy manner in which he has continued the illustrious traditions of the Spanish drama

laureates.name: Anatole France
laureates.motivation: in recognition of his brilliant literary achievements, characterized as they are by a nobility of style, a profound human sympathy, grace, and a true Gallic temperament

laureates.name: Knut Hamsun
laureates.motivation: for his monumental work, Growth of the Soil

laureates.name: Carl Spitteler
laureates.motivation: in special appreciation of his epic, Olympian Spring

1918.notes: No Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded this year due to World War I

laureates.name: Karl Gjellerup
laureates.motivation: for his varied and rich poetry, which is inspired by lofty ideals

laureates.name: Henrik Pontoppidan
laureates.motivation: for his authentic descriptions of present-day life in Denmark

laureates.name: Verner von Heidenstam
laureates.motivation: in recognition of his significance as the leading representative of a new era in our literature

laureates.name: Romain Rolland
laureates.motivation: as a tribute to the lofty idealism of his literary production and to the sympathy and love of truth with which he has described different types of human beings

1914.notes: No Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded this year due to World War I

laureates.name: Rabindranath Tagore
laureates.motivation: because of his profoundly sensitive, fresh and beautiful verse, by which, with consummate skill, he has made his poetic thought, expressed in his own English words, a part of the literature of the West

laureates.name: Gerhart Hauptmann
laureates.motivation: primarily in recognition of his fruitful, varied and outstanding production in the realm of dramatic art

laureates.name: Maurice Maeterlinck
laureates.motivation: in appreciation of his many-sided literary activities, and especially of his dramatic works, which are distinguished by a wealth of imagination and by a poetic fancy, which reveals, sometimes in the guise of a fairy tale, a deep inspiration, while in a mysterious way they appeal to the readers' own feelings and stimulate their imaginations

laureates.name: Paul Heyse
laureates.motivation: as a tribute to the consummate artistry, permeated with idealism, which he has demonstrated during his long productive career as a lyric poet, dramatist, novelist and writer of world-renowned short stories

laureates.name: Selma Lagerlöf
laureates.motivation: in appreciation of the lofty idealism, vivid imagination and spiritual perception that characterize her writings

laureates.name: Rudolf Eucken
laureates.motivation: in recognition of his earnest search for truth, his penetrating power of thought, his wide range of vision, and the warmth and strength in presentation with which in his numerous works he has vindicated and developed an idealistic philosophy of life

laureates.name: Rudyard Kipling
laureates.motivation: in consideration of the power of observation, originality of imagination, virility of ideas and remarkable talent for narration that characterize the creations of this world-famous author

laureates.name: Giosuè Carducci
laureates.motivation: not only in consideration of his deep learning and critical research, but above all as a tribute to the creative energy, freshness of style, and lyrical force which characterize his poetic masterpieces

laureates.name: Henryk Sienkiewicz
laureates.motivation: because of his outstanding merits as an epic writer

laureates.name: Frédéric Mistral
laureates.motivation: in recognition of the fresh originality and true inspiration of his poetic production, which faithfully reflects the natural scenery and native spirit of his people, and, in addition, his significant work as a Provençal philologist

laureates.name: José Echegaray
laureates.motivation: in recognition of the numerous and brilliant compositions which, in an individual and original manner, have revived the great traditions of the Spanish drama

laureates.name: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson
laureates.motivation: as a tribute to his noble, magnificent and versatile poetry, which has always been distinguished by both the freshness of its inspiration and the rare purity of its spirit

laureates.name: Theodor Mommsen
laureates.motivation: the greatest living master of the art of historical writing, with special reference to his monumental work A History of Rome

laureates.name: Sully Prudhomme
laureates.motivation: in special recognition of his poetic composition, which gives evidence of lofty idealism, artistic perfection and a rare combination of the qualities of both heart and intellect
